title: My Lady's Gown, There's Gairs Upon'T.
content:
Tune - "Gregg's Pipes."



I.

        My lady's gown, there's gairs upon't,
        And gowden flowers sae rare upon't;
        But Jenny's jimps and jirkinet,
        My lord thinks meikle mair upon't.
        My lord a-hunting he is gane,
        But hounds or hawks wi' him are nane;
        By Colin's cottage lies his game,
        If Colin's Jenny be at hame.

II.

        My lady's white, my lady's red,
        And kith and kin o' Cassillis' blude;
        But her ten-pund lands o' tocher guid
        Were a' the charms his lordship lo'ed.

III.

        Out o'er yon muir, out o'er yon moss,
        Whare gor-cocks thro' the heather pass,
        There wons auld Colin's bonnie lass,
        A lily in a wilderness.

IV.

        Sae sweetly move her genty limbs,
        Like music notes o' lovers' hymns:
        The diamond dew is her een sae blue,
        Where laughing love sae wanton swims.

V.

        My lady's dink, my lady's drest,
        The flower and fancy o' the west;
        But the lassie that a man lo'es best,
        O that's the lass to make him blest.
        My lady's gown, there's gairs upon't,
        And gowden flowers sae rare upon't;
        But Jenny's jimps and jirkinet,
        My lord thinks meikle mair upon't.
meaning:
"My Lady's Gown, There's Gairs Upon'T." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
